steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: The goal is to provide a detailed overview of the structures at Keet Seel. The notes give five facts: the civilization, the location and dates, the cliff alcove setting, the multistory adobe and stone buildings with cube-shaped contiguous rooms, and the kivas (round ceremonial rooms). A detailed structural overview must cover the physical features of the buildings—not just the history, builders, or dates.

steps.title: Eliminate choices that miss the goal, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Located in northeastern Arizona, Keet Seel was designed with kivas, round rooms that were typically used for ceremonial purposes and as gathering spaces.': Mentions only kivas and the location—omits the adobe/stone construction, multistory buildings, and cube-shaped rooms. Partial at best. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Keet Seel, whose structures were built by Ancestral Puebloans in northeastern Arizona, was inhabited from approximately 1250–1300 CE.': Focuses on who built it and when—provides no structural detail at all. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Constructed in a natural cliff alcove, Keet Seel's multistory adobe and stone buildings featured round rooms called kivas surrounded by cube-shaped, contiguous rooms.': Covers the cliff alcove setting, multistory construction, building materials, kivas, and contiguous cube-shaped rooms—a comprehensive structural overview. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Ancestral Puebloans used adobe and stone in the construction of Keet Seel in northeastern Arizona.': Names only the materials and builders—leaves out the alcove, multistory layout, kivas, and room arrangement. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
